title: Salesforce Automation Flow
description: A Salesforce Flow definition representing an automation built with Flow Builder. Flows are the primary declarative automation tool in Salesforce, replacing Workflow Rules and Process Builder. They support screen flows (user-interactive), autolaunched flows (triggered automatically), scheduled flows, record-triggered flows, and platform event-triggered flows. This schema models the Flow metadata as returned by the Tooling API and Metadata API.

Id.description: The 18-character Salesforce record ID for this specific Flow version. Each version of a Flow has a unique ID.

DefinitionId.description: The 18-character ID of the parent FlowDefinition record. All versions of the same flow share this DefinitionId.

DeveloperName.description: The unique developer name (API name) for this flow. Used as the identifier in metadata deployments and API references. Cannot contain spaces or special characters.

MasterLabel.description: The display label shown in the Flow Builder UI and flow list views.

Description.description: An optional description of the flow's purpose and behavior.

ProcessType.description: The type of flow, which determines how and when it executes. AutoLaunchedFlow runs without user interaction (triggered by records, schedules, or invocable actions). Flow is a Screen Flow that presents UI screens to users. Workflow represents legacy Process Builder processes. Other types serve specialized purposes.

TriggerType.description: For record-triggered flows, specifies when the flow runs relative to the DML operation.

TriggerObject.description: For record-triggered flows, the API name of the SObject that triggers the flow (e.g., Account, Contact, Opportunity, CustomObject__c).

RecordTriggerType.description: For record-triggered flows, specifies which record operations trigger the flow.

Status.description: The status of this flow version. Active means this version is the currently running version. Draft is a version under development. Obsolete is a previously active version that has been superseded. InvalidDraft is a draft with validation errors.

VersionNumber.description: The version number within the FlowDefinition. Increments with each new version. The active version number is stored on the FlowDefinition record.

ApiVersion.description: The Salesforce API version this flow was created with (e.g., 63.0). Determines which flow features are available.

RunInMode.description: The context in which the flow runs. SystemModeWithSharing runs with full object/field access but respects sharing rules. SystemModeWithoutSharing runs with full access and no sharing restrictions. DefaultMode runs in the context of the running user.

Metadata.description: The full flow metadata containing all elements, connectors, variables, and configuration. This is the complete definition of the flow's logic.

interviewLabel.description: The label for flow interviews (running instances) shown in Setup

startElementReference.description: The API name of the first element in the flow. Execution begins here.

variables.description: Variables used to store data during flow execution

formulas.description: Formula expressions calculated during flow execution

decisions.description: Decision elements that branch flow execution based on conditions

assignments.description: Assignment elements that set variable values

recordCreates.description: Elements that create new Salesforce records

recordUpdates.description: Elements that update existing Salesforce records

loops.description: Loop elements that iterate over collections

screens.description: Screen elements that display UI to users (Screen Flows only)

actionCalls.description: Elements that invoke actions (Apex, email alerts, flows, etc.)

subflows.description: Elements that invoke other flows as subflows

waits.description: Wait elements that pause flow execution until conditions are met

FullName.description: The fully qualified name including namespace prefix if applicable (e.g., MyNamespace__MyFlow-1)

IsTemplate.description: Whether this flow is a template that can be used to create new flows

IsOverridable.description: Whether this flow can be overridden by a flow in a subscriber org

CreatedDate.description: The date and time this flow version was created, in ISO 8601 format

CreatedById.description: The 18-character ID of the user who created this flow version

LastModifiedDate.description: The date and time this flow version was last modified

LastModifiedById.description: The 18-character ID of the user who last modified this flow version

FlowStart.title: Flow Start Element
FlowStart.description: The start element configuration for the flow, defining trigger conditions and scheduling.

doesRequireRecordChangedToMeetCriteria.description: For record-triggered flows, whether the record must newly meet the filter criteria (not just continue to meet them)

filterLogic.description: Custom filter logic (e.g., '1 AND (2 OR 3)') for combining filter conditions

filters.description: Filter conditions that determine which records trigger the flow

object.description: The SObject type for record-triggered flows

FlowVariable.title: Flow Variable
FlowVariable.description: A variable that stores data during flow execution. Variables can hold single values, collections, or SObject records.

name.description: The API name of the variable

dataType.description: The data type of the variable

isCollection.description: Whether this variable holds a collection (list) of values

isInput.description: Whether this variable is available as an input parameter when the flow is invoked

isOutput.description: Whether this variable is available as an output parameter after the flow completes

objectType.description: For SObject variables, the API name of the SObject type

value.description: The default value for this variable

FlowFormula.title: Flow Formula
FlowFormula.description: A formula expression evaluated at runtime. Uses the same formula syntax as Salesforce formula fields.

FlowDecision.title: Flow Decision
FlowDecision.description: A decision element that evaluates conditions and branches to different paths. Similar to an if/else-if/else construct.

defaultConnector.description: The path taken when no rule conditions are met (the 'else' path)

conditionLogic.description: How conditions are combined: 'and', 'or', or custom (e.g., '1 AND (2 OR 3)')

FlowAssignment.title: Flow Assignment
FlowAssignment.description: An assignment element that sets the value of one or more variables.

assignToReference.description: The variable to assign a value to

FlowRecordCreate.title: Flow Record Create
FlowRecordCreate.description: An element that creates one or more Salesforce records.

inputReference.description: Reference to an SObject variable containing the record to create

assignRecordIdToReference.description: Variable to store the ID of the newly created record

faultConnector.description: Path taken when the create operation fails

FlowRecordUpdate.title: Flow Record Update
FlowRecordUpdate.description: An element that updates Salesforce records matching specified criteria.

object.description: The SObject type to update (when using filter criteria)

inputReference.description: Reference to an SObject variable or collection containing records to update

filters.description: Filter criteria to identify which records to update

FlowRecordDelete.title: Flow Record Delete
FlowRecordDelete.description: An element that deletes Salesforce records matching specified criteria.

FlowRecordLookup.title: Flow Record Lookup
FlowRecordLookup.description: An element that queries Salesforce records and stores the results in variables.

getFirstRecordOnly.description: If true, only the first matching record is returned

storeOutputAutomatically.description: If true, results are automatically stored in auto-generated variables

outputReference.description: Variable to store the query results

queriedFields.description: Fields to retrieve from matching records

FlowLoop.title: Flow Loop
FlowLoop.description: A loop element that iterates over a collection variable, executing contained elements for each item.

collectionReference.description: The collection variable to iterate over

nextValueConnector.description: Path to the first element inside the loop

noMoreValuesConnector.description: Path taken after all items have been iterated

FlowScreen.title: Flow Screen
FlowScreen.description: A screen element that presents a UI to the user in a Screen Flow.

fieldType.description: The type of screen component (e.g., InputField, DisplayText, RadioButtons, ComponentInstance)

FlowActionCall.title: Flow Action Call
FlowActionCall.description: An element that invokes an action such as an Apex method, email alert, quick action, or external service.

actionName.description: The API name of the action to invoke

actionType.description: The type of action (e.g., apex, emailAlert, quickAction, flow, externalService, submit, chatterPost)

FlowSubflow.title: Flow Subflow
FlowSubflow.description: An element that invokes another flow as a subflow.

flowName.description: The developer name of the flow to invoke

FlowWait.title: Flow Wait
FlowWait.description: A wait element that pauses flow execution until one or more conditions are met (time-based or platform event).

eventType.description: The type of wait condition (e.g., AlarmEvent, PlatformEventMessage)

FlowConnector.title: Flow Connector
FlowConnector.description: A connector that links one flow element to the next, defining the execution path.

targetReference.description: The API name of the target element this connector leads to

FlowCondition.title: Flow Condition
FlowCondition.description: A single condition used in decisions, filters, and wait events.

leftValueReference.description: Reference to the left-hand value (e.g., a variable or field reference)

rightValue.description: The right-hand value to compare against

FlowRecordFilter.title: Flow Record Filter
FlowRecordFilter.description: A filter condition used in record-triggered flow starts, record lookups, record updates, and record deletes.

field.description: The API name of the field to filter on

FlowInputFieldAssignment.title: Flow Input Field Assignment
FlowInputFieldAssignment.description: Maps a value to a field on a record being created or updated, or to an input parameter.

field.description: The API name of the field to set

FlowElementValue.title: Flow Element Value
FlowElementValue.description: A value reference used throughout flow definitions. Can be a literal value, a variable reference, or an element reference.

elementReference.description: Reference to a flow variable, formula, or other element
